panels.title: Server Request Duration p50/p95/p99
panels.description: Shows median, 95th, and 99th percentile server-side request durations. Rising percentiles indicate backend processing slowdowns that may degrade user experience.

panels.title: Request Body Size p50/p95/p99
panels.description: Shows median, 95th, and 99th percentile inbound request payload sizes. Unexpectedly large payloads can increase serialization cost and memory pressure.

panels.title: Response Body Size p50/p95/p99
panels.description: Shows median, 95th, and 99th percentile outbound response payload sizes. Growing response sizes can increase network egress cost and client rendering time.

panels.title: HTTP Request Rate by Status Code
panels.description: Plots inbound request rate over time, split by HTTP status code. Watch for growing non-2xx lines that diverge from the success baseline.

panels.title: Active HTTP Requests
panels.description: Tracks the number of in-flight HTTP requests over time. Sustained climbs without a corresponding RPS increase suggest requests are queuing or stalling.

panels.title: Top Routes by Request Volume
panels.description: Ranks HTTP routes by total request count to highlight the busiest endpoints. Use this to identify hot paths that benefit most from optimization.

panels.title: Top Routes by p95 Latency
panels.description: Ranks HTTP routes by their 95th-percentile response time. Routes at the top are the slowest and may need query optimization or caching.

panels.title: Error Rate by Route
panels.description: Compares error percentages across HTTP routes. Routes with elevated error rates relative to peers signal localized reliability problems.

panels.title: Route Error Rate Over Time
panels.description: Plots per-route error counts over time to reveal which routes are contributing to error spikes. Diverging lines help isolate the offending endpoint.

panels.title: HTTP Status Distribution
panels.description: Compares the share of 2xx, 3xx, 4xx, and 5xx responses as a proportion of total traffic. A healthy distribution is dominated by the 2xx segment.

panels.title: 5xx Errors by Route & Service
panels.description: Lists each route-service pair with its 5xx error count, sorted by volume. Rows near the top are the highest-impact targets for reliability fixes.

panels.title: Overall HTTP Error Rate Over Time
panels.description: Plots the aggregate HTTP error rate as a time series. Spikes that correlate with deployments or traffic changes help pinpoint the root cause of failures.

panels.title: Outbound HTTP Duration p50/p95/p99
panels.description: Shows median, 95th, and 99th percentile durations for outbound HTTP calls. A widening gap between p50 and p99 signals growing tail-latency variance in external dependencies.

panels.title: DNS Lookup & TLS Handshake Duration
panels.description: Shows time spent on DNS resolution and TLS handshakes for outbound calls. Elevated values may point to DNS infrastructure issues or certificate negotiation overhead.

panels.title: Top External Hosts by Volume
panels.description: Ranks external hosts by outbound request count. Dominance by a single host may indicate tight coupling or a candidate for caching.

panels.title: External Host p95 Latency
panels.description: Compares 95th-percentile response time across external hosts. Hosts with disproportionately high latency may be bottlenecking end-to-end request flow.

panels.title: External Host Error Rate
panels.description: Compares error percentages across external hosts. A host with a disproportionately high error rate may need circuit-breaking or fallback logic.
